summary: Verstappen hunts redemption at Spa after two successive high-speed crashes force Red Bull to abandon their experimental rear wing and revert to conventional downforce. Norris's 10-place grid penalty catapults him into a recovery mission from deep in the field, potentially reshaping McLaren's strategy as they chase constructors' championship momentum. A strong result here could signal whether Hamilton's Spanish victory has genuinely reignited Ferrari's title challenge or merely papered over deeper structural cracks.
